fields.definition_origin: Pattern codified by Appcues 2018-2019 ('Mobile Permission Priming' guides), refined by Plotline 2024 product blog, and benchmarked by Pushwoosh 2025 push-opt-in studies. Distinct from item 24 (Permission Priming PATTERN — the broader pre-permission discipline). This item is the SCREEN ARTIFACT itself — the specific UI that fires immediately before the native OS permission dialog. Apple's ATT framework (App Tracking Transparency, iOS 14.5+ 2021-04) and Apple's 2024-2025 HIG explicitly recommend a pre-permission soft-ask. Google Play has no formal recommendation but Material 3 Expressive (2025) treats permission requests as first-class flow events.
fields.core_mechanism: Mechanism stack: (1) Reciprocity (Cialdini 1984) — the soft-ask screen DELIVERS context/value before asking, creating a small reciprocity debt the user repays by tapping 'Allow'. (2) Foot-in-the-door (Freedman & Fraser 1966) — tapping 'Continue' on the soft-ask is a low-cost commitment that primes 'Allow' on the native dialog. (3) Anchoring — the user's first encounter with the permission concept is in the app's voice + brand, not the OS's cold modal. (4) Uncertainty reduction — explaining WHY the permission is needed (e.g., 'so we can remind you at your chosen time') resolves the user's privacy-evaluation anxiety upfront, reducing native-dialog rejection.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Pre-frontal evaluation (DLPFC) of permission requests is heavily mediated by trust signals. The soft-ask screen primes oxytocin-mediated trust circuitry by using the app's already-established voice; the cold native dialog activates threat-detection (amygdala) instead.
fields.empirical_evidence: Pushwoosh 2025 push-opt-in benchmarks: iOS apps WITH soft-ask screen achieve 60-80% opt-in vs ~25% baseline without; Android apps WITH soft-ask achieve 85-92% vs ~55% baseline. Plotline 2025 case studies: 2-3× opt-in lift documented across wellness, productivity, AI-wrapper categories. Appcues 2024 study (N=12 apps): 20% incremental opt-in lift from a custom value-explanation screen vs generic copy. Adapty 2026 ATT data: pre-permission soft-ask before ATT native dialog lifts ATT opt-in 2-3× over cold ATT dialog. Apple Developer 2024 documentation explicitly recommends contextual pre-permission UI.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: soft_ask_view → soft_ask_continue rate; soft_ask_continue → native_dialog_allow rate; net opt-in rate (% of soft_ask_views that result in OS-level permission granted); abandonment rate at soft-ask; time-on-soft-ask.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: iOS push opt-in: 60-80% with soft-ask vs ~25% baseline (Pushwoosh 2025). Android: 85-92% vs ~55% baseline. ATT opt-in: 2-3× lift with soft-ask (Adapty 2026). Custom value-explanation copy: +20% incremental over generic (Appcues 2024).
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Architecture: bottom-sheet modal (iOS) or full-screen modal (Android) presented BEFORE calling the permission API. The 'Allow' button on the soft-ask triggers the native dialog; 'Not now' defers without firing native (preserves ability to retry later — once a user denies the native dialog, retrying is much harder). (2) Copy formula: '[Value to user]. We'll [specific thing]. You can change this anytime in Settings.' Example: 'Get a single gentle reminder at your chosen time. We'll send one push at 8pm. You can change this anytime in Settings.' (3) Visual: large icon (bell for push, location-pin for location, etc.) at archetype-aligned style (NOT generic Lucide); brief headline; bullet list of 2-3 benefits; primary CTA 'Allow' (or 'Continue' which feels softer); secondary 'Not now' (NOT 'Cancel' which feels destructive). (4) Placement in FSM: AFTER value has been demonstrated (after AI Plan Reveal, after Mid-Onboarding Review prompt). Cold pre-permission at step 1 = waste — user hasn't earned trust to spend. (5) Order matters per item 24 Permission Ladder: push primer → soft-ask → native dialog, THEN ATT (separate soft-ask), THEN feature-gated permissions. (6) Persistence: store `soft_ask_outcome` per permission in MMKV; re-show defer-and-retry after 7+ days only if user hasn't granted via Settings.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step ~31: bell-icon soft-ask 'Reminders to log your meals' with 3 bullet benefits, 'Allow' CTA; net iOS opt-in ~75% per teardowns. (2) Headspace 2024: soft-ask after personalized plan reveal with 'Stay consistent' value framing; opt-in ~80%. (3) Calm 2024: chronotype-aware soft-ask ('We'll send your evening practice reminder at 8pm') with 'Allow' / 'Not now' affordances. (4) Duolingo: soft-ask combined with chronotype question ('What time of day works best?'); opt-in ~85%. (5) Strava: location-permission soft-ask with map preview showing what tracking enables (visual demo of value). (6) Acorns: notifications soft-ask focused on transactional value ('Round-up confirmations and balance alerts'). (7) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: soft-ask for push framed as 'A single gentle prayer reminder at your chosen time' (Caregiver-archetype voice). (8) ATT-specific: TikTok ATT soft-ask explains 'personalized ads' value-trade; Instagram ATT soft-ask similar. (9) Plotline-built bottom-sheet pattern: standardized component used by 100+ mobile apps.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary pattern. iOS: SDK-level access to permission state (`UNUserNotificationCenter`, `ATTrackingManager`, `CLLocationManager`). Android: `requestPermissions()` runtime permissions API. Web equivalent: similar bottom-sheet pattern before `Notification.requestPermission()` or `navigator.geolocation` — but web push opt-in is structurally lower regardless (~5-15%) so soft-ask has less compounding benefit.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — bottom-sheet with translucent material affordance feels native to the soft-ask role; bolder left-aligned headline matches Liquid Glass alert typography. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) — bottom-sheet with expressive shape system + motion springs handles the entry/exit elegantly.
fields.category_fit: UNIVERSAL — every permission-using category benefits from soft-ask. STRONGEST: wellness/habit (push-permission gate is critical — Calm, Headspace, Cal AI, Duolingo, Prayer Lock), productivity (push for reminders), social (push for messages), fintech (push for transaction confirmations). ACCEPTABLE: AI-wrapper (push for new feature reveals), edtech, fitness. Apply per-permission: push, ATT (iOS only), location, contacts, camera, photos, health, biometric. Each permission gets its own soft-ask.
fields.tooling_options: OneSignal Deferred Permission Prompt (built-in soft-ask SDK), Plotline (full-flow visual builder + bottom-sheet templates), native bottom-sheet (iOS UISheetPresentationController, Android BottomSheetDialogFragment, RN @gorhom/bottom-sheet), expo-notifications + custom modal, react-native-permissions for permission state management. For ATT: Apple's ATTrackingManager + custom pre-permission UI.
fields.ai_dependency: none. Soft-ask is a UI pattern, not an AI feature. (LLM could generate per-user value framing but generic copy works fine.)
fields.claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a pre-permission soft-ask bottom-sheet for push notifications. Layout: top — large bell icon at archetype-aligned style (NOT generic Lucide bell), 64pt brand-color. Middle — 28pt Cabinet Grotesk headline left-aligned: "Get a gentle reminder at your chosen time." Body — 16pt Manrope: "We''ll send one push at 8pm. You can change anytime in Settings." 3 bullet benefits in 14pt Manrope. Bottom — primary CTA "Allow" (full-width 56pt, brand color, sensoryFeedback .impact on press) → fires `requestNotificationPermission()`. Secondary "Not now" (text button, neutral). FSM placement: after AI Plan Reveal, after Mid-Onboarding Review.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Forbid: cold native dialog without soft-ask preceding it. Forbid generic Lucide bell icon. Forbid "Cancel" (use "Not now"). Forbid soft-ask at step 1 of onboarding (no trust earned). Forbid copy that doesn''t name what value the user gets.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a pre-permission soft-ask bottom sheet for [category] app push notification. Bottom-sheet pattern (slide up from bottom, 70% screen height). Top: large 64pt brand-color bell icon (custom or Phosphor Duotone, NOT generic Lucide). Headline 28pt Cabinet Grotesk left-aligned: "Get a gentle reminder at your chosen time." Body 16pt Manrope: "We''ll send one push at 8pm. You can change anytime in Settings." 3 bullet checkmarks (NOT emoji) with brand-aligned typographic markers. Bottom thumb-zone: full-width 56pt primary CTA "Allow" in brand OKLCH. Below: text-button "Not now" in muted gray. Forbid Inter / Lucide bell / "Don''t miss out!" copy / "Cancel" button.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) skips the soft-ask entirely and fires the native dialog cold (loses 30-50 points of opt-in), (2) uses generic Lucide bell icon (slop signature + accessibility-hostile small target), (3) uses "Cancel" button copy (feels destructive — actual users tap and leave the flow), (4) places soft-ask at step 1 (no trust earned, lower opt-in than waiting), (5) uses vague benefit copy ("Stay updated!" — meaningless), (6) makes the soft-ask a full-screen blocker (overwhelming) instead of bottom-sheet, (7) doesn't separate push vs ATT vs location asks (cold-stacks all three permissions in one screen — guaranteed denials). Counter-prompt: 'Mandatory: bottom-sheet soft-ask before EVERY native permission dialog, presented AFTER value demonstrated. Each permission gets its own soft-ask — never stack. Use "Not now" not "Cancel". Specific value framing in copy (name what the permission enables for the user). Forbid Lucide bell icon — use archetype-aligned visual.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Cold native dialog without soft-ask preceding it (loses 30-50 points of opt-in). (2) Soft-ask at step 1 (no trust earned). (3) Stacking multiple permissions in one soft-ask (guaranteed cumulative denial). (4) Generic 'Stay updated!' copy without specific value. (5) 'Cancel' button instead of 'Not now' (destructive framing). (6) Forced re-show of soft-ask after user said 'Not now' (sludge). (7) Lucide generic bell icon (slop). (8) Pre-permission for ATT framed as 'allow tracking' (negative anchor) instead of 'allow personalized plan recommendations' (value anchor). (9) Pre-permission for camera/health framed as the technical permission instead of the user feature it enables.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low: standard UX pattern, no regulatory issue. ATT-specific: Apple 2024-2025 guidance allows pre-permission soft-ask if it doesn't manipulate consent (no fake urgency, no confirm-shaming). EU GDPR: explicit consent applies, pre-permission is consistent with consent best practice. EU DFA Q4 2026 — pre-permission soft-ask is the OPPOSITE of dark pattern; protected under DFA. NEVER use pre-permission to dark-pattern users into permissions they wouldn't otherwise grant (DFA-flagged).
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple ATT framework — soft-ask before ATT native dialog allowed and recommended, but copy must NOT manipulate (no '4M users allowed tracking' fake social proof; no 'fix tracking error' deceptive framing — Apple rejects on review). Apple Guideline 5.1.1 — permission requests must clearly explain why; soft-ask is the canonical place to do this. Android target API 35 (deadline 2025-08-31) — runtime permissions discipline.
fields.accessibility_implications: Bottom-sheet must be VoiceOver/TalkBack-accessible (live-region announcement on present). 'Allow' / 'Not now' buttons ≥ 44pt iOS / 48dp Android. Touch targets ≥ 24×24 px (WCAG 2.2 § 2.5.8). Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1. Dynamic Type up to 200% reflow. Focus appearance on 'Allow' (2px outline). Reduced-motion replaces sheet-slide with crossfade. Keyboard / switch-control reachable.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Once user denies the native dialog, retrying is much harder (iOS requires user to go to Settings; Android shows 'Don't ask again'). Therefore 'Not now' MUST defer the native call (don't fire it yet). (2) Permission state can be system-revoked at any time — re-check before using the permission, not just at install. (3) Locale-specific copy required — 'Allow' and 'Not now' translations vary; native copywriters per locale. (4) Cold start performance — soft-ask must render in <100ms (don't block on async loads). (5) Context-loss bug — if soft-ask presents during background app refresh, it can fire on a non-foreground session and feel jarring.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Universal applicability with locale tweaks. JP: more direct value framing preferred ('Receive a daily prayer at 6am'); social-proof framing reduces opt-in. KR/CN: trust signals matter more — include 'verified by [trust marker]' inline. LATAM: warmer brand voice ('Tu recordatorio diario') outperforms transactional. MENA: prayer-time aware framing for spiritual apps (Mau Baron Prayer Lock localizes to prayer times). Avoid: generic 'Don't miss out!' urgency copy in JP/DE markets (reads aggressive); use value-led framing.
fields.prescribed_action: For EVERY OS-level permission request, present a bottom-sheet soft-ask BEFORE firing the native dialog. Place after value demonstrated (after AI Plan Reveal at minimum, ideally after Mid-Onboarding Review). Copy formula: '[Value to user]. We''ll [specific thing]. You can change anytime in Settings.' Primary CTA 'Allow' fires native; secondary 'Not now' defers without firing. Each permission gets its own soft-ask — never stack. Use archetype-aligned visual (NOT generic Lucide). Locale-specific native copywriters required.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Soft-ask reuses identity-question answers for value framing — 'Send your morning practice reminder at the time you said' (cross-step stitching from item 14).
fields.aha_relevance: Soft-ask placement AFTER AI Plan Reveal compounds the peak — user is in maximum-trust state when the permission is requested. Pre-Aha soft-asks lose ~20 points of opt-in.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver: gentle, no urgency, 'when you're ready' adjacent. Magician: framed as part of transformation arc ('Receive your daily reveal'). Sage: explanation-led, transparent. Jester: playful but specific (no 'Don't miss out!' which is Jester slop). Outlaw: fits if framed against the OS's cold dialog ('We hate cold dialogs too — here's why we want to send you reminders'). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json archetype voice.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Contextless Permissions (item from Six-Antipattern Audit) — soft-ask IS the counter to contextless permissions. Pairs against Wall-of-Text — keep copy tight (3 bullet benefits max). Aligns with Permission Priming Pattern (item 24 — broader concept), Notification Ladder (item 59 — soft-ask is Day 0 step), Cross-Step Personalization Stitching (item 14 — reuse user inputs in soft-ask copy).
sources: [Plotline — How to Improve Push Notification Opt-in Rates](https://www.plotline.so/blog/how-to-improve-push-notification-opt-in-rates)
[Anstrex — Ultimate Guide to Push Notification Consent 2025](https://www.anstrex.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-push-notification-consent-in-2025)
